vulnerability.reference: Self-attention gradients retain token-level positional information, enabling verbatim text reconstruction.
vulnerability.generated: Self-attention layer gradients preserve exact token order and identity, making shared updates directly invertible into training text.

impact.reference: Reconstructed private communications expose intimate conversations and trade secrets.
impact.generated: Full reconstruction of private training sequences, breaching confidentiality of sensitive language data across all clients.

dose_response.reference: Reconstructing text tokens from self-attention gradients in federated LLM training exposes personal communications, enabling social engineering and incurring GDPR penalties, harming user privacy and trust.
dose_response.generated: Gradient inversion attacks, via clients fine-tuning LLMs in federated learning, trigger self-attention layer gradients that preserve exact token order and identity, leading to full reconstruction of private training sequences and breaching confidentiality.
